content: The coordination dynamic between the prefrontal cortex and hippocampus during human memory retrieval can be borrowed to redesign the memory architecture of state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, where the prefrontal region maps to a goal-directed gating module that controls what episodic memories the hippocampus-aligned memory bank retrieves for active query selection. This gating mechanism naturally resolves the working memory capacity limit that plagues current active learning agents during long-horizon interactive tasks.
